monthly.2016-04: Eastside King edged out the Pacific Northwest pack with 140 good-outdoor hours in April 2016, while Charlotte County trailed at 97 as Florida heat pushed temps past the comfort zone.
monthly.2016-05: Snohomish and Eastside King counties tied for the win with 406 and 404 good-outdoor hours in May 2016, while Charlotte County, FL trailed at 284, dragged down by heat, heavy rain, and blistering UV.
monthly.2016-06: Eastside King edged Snohomish by just 4 hours (403 vs 399) for June 2016's best outdoor weather, while Santa Clara and Charlotte trailed — done in by heat and Florida rain, respectively.
monthly.2016-07: Eastside King edged Snohomish in a near-tie for July 2016's best outdoor hours (88% vs 87% of daytime), while Virginia and Florida trailed thanks to oppressive heat and heavy rain.
monthly.2016-08: Snohomish County, WA edged Eastside King by just six hours for Aug 2016's best outdoor weather, with both Washington counties trouncing the rest thanks to cooler temps and milder UV.
monthly.2016-09: Snohomish edged Eastside King in a near-tie for September 2016's best outdoor hours (both ~92% of daytime), while Santa Clara trailed the pack as heat and stronger UV ate into its window.
monthly.2016-10: Albemarle County, VA topped October 2016 with 313 good-outdoor hours (92% of daytime), while the Washington counties trailed thanks to a soggy 260+ mm of rain.
monthly.2016-11: Charlotte County, FL took November with 314 good-outdoor hours, edging out the Bay Area pair while Pacific Northwest counties trailed as 200+ mm of rain washed out Eastside King and Snohomish.
monthly.2016-12: Charlotte County, FL ran away with December 2016 at 310 good-outdoor hours — every daytime hour qualified — while Washington's cold, soggy Eastside King and Snohomish barely cleared 20.
monthly.2017-01: Charlotte County, FL ran away with January 2017 at 313 good-outdoor hours (97% of daytime), while the Pacific Northwest froze out — Eastside King and Snohomish barely cleared 40 hours thanks to cold.
monthly.2017-02: Charlotte County, FL and San Francisco tied at 258 good-outdoor hours, while Eastside King and Snohomish languished near 60 — cold Pacific Northwest temps shut the door.
monthly.2017-03: San Francisco and Santa Clara tied at 319 outdoor-friendly hours in March 2017, far ahead of soggy Eastside King (210h), where 252mm of rain drowned the month.
monthly.2017-04: Snohomish County edged out the pack with 338 good-outdoor hours in April 2017, while Charlotte County, FL trailed at 250 — too hot and UV-heavy for comfort.
monthly.2017-05: Eastside King led May 2017 with 386 good-outdoor hours (83% of daylight), edging Snohomish by a nose, while Charlotte FL trailed at 245h — too hot and UV-harsh.
monthly.2017-06: Snohomish County edged out the pack with 407 outdoor-friendly hours in June 2017, while Charlotte County's 274 mm of rain and Santa Clara's 38°C heat dragged the sunbelt to the bottom.
monthly.2017-07: Eastside King edged out Snohomish (355 vs. 352 good-outdoor hours) to lead July 2017, while Santa Clara trailed at 217, hammered by high-UV afternoons and 36°C peaks.
monthly.2017-08: Snohomish County, WA edged out the pack with 329 good-outdoor hours in Aug 2017, while Charlotte County, FL trailed at 219h thanks to 440mm of rain.
monthly.2017-09: Snohomish County, WA took the Sep 2017 crown with 335 good-outdoor hours, edging nearby King by 12 hours as heat and fierce UV dragged California and Florida down.
monthly.2017-10: San Francisco edged out the pack with 306 good-outdoor hours in October 2017, while soggy Eastside King trailed at 280 — blame 171 mm of Pacific Northwest rain.

monthly.2017-12: Charlotte County, FL ran away with it at 309 outdoor-friendly hours — every daytime hour qualified — while the Pacific Northwest got drenched, with Snohomish stuck at just 32 hours under 131mm of rain.

range_overview.last1y: San Francisco stole the year with 3,685 good-outdoor hours — 83% of daylight — while Charlotte County, Florida trailed at 2,873, its subtropical heat peaking at 37°C routinely pushing afternoons out of the comfort zone.
range_overview.last5y: San Francisco's famously mild fog-cooled coast led the pack with 82% of daytime hours fit for outdoors, while soggy, subtropical Charlotte County, Florida trailed at 67% — drowned out by nearly seven meters of rain and relentless heat.
range_overview.last10y: San Francisco's foggy chill paid off with a decade-leading 36,023 good-outdoor hours (81% of daytime), while Albemarle County trailed at 28,615 (64%), weighed down by sticky Virginia summers pushing past comfortable UV and heat.
